originalTitle: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
summary: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in the […]
content: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in the last two weeks. The shootings in Maine and Texas were just the latest civilian deaths at the hands of ICE agents, who have attempted to intimidate witnesses following its recent spate of high-profile killings. The new guidance would end FBI investigations of assaults against DHS agents. The government has aggressively pursued cases against protesters and others who have had even fleeting and unintentional physical contact with agents. But those felony assault cases routinely fall apart, with nearly half of them ending with an acquittal, the charges being thrown out, or withdrawn, according to a Times analysis. While the FBI investigations are ostensibly to help the government make a case against protesters and others, they often provide evidence against the officers that could be used in future trials against them. According to The Times: > Under the change, responsibility for investigating cases involving federal immigration officers would likely fall to Homeland Security Investigations, an arm of ICE, effectively leaving the agency to investigate itself…Homeland Security Investigations does not have jurisdiction to investigate civil rights violations, so putting the change into practice would make it far less likely that shootings of unarmed people by ICE agents would be investigated to determine if the agents broke federal law.
chineseTitle: 据报道，FBI 将不再调查 ICE
chineseSummary: 据《纽约时报》报道，联邦特工已被告知，联邦调查局将不再调查涉及 ICE 特工的对抗事件。国土安全部和司法部向《泰晤士报》否认了政策的改变。据报道，指导方针发生了变化，此前 ICE 特工对暴力行为进行了重新审查，这些特工在 […]
chineseContent: 据《纽约时报》报道，联邦特工已被告知，联邦调查局将不再调查涉及 ICE 特工的对抗事件。国土安全部和司法部向《泰晤士报》否认了政策的改变。据报道，指导意见发生了变化，此前 ICE 特工对过去两周杀害两人的暴力行为进行了重新审查。缅因州和得克萨斯州的枪击事件只是 ICE 特工造成的最新一起平民死亡事件，这些特工在最近发生了一系列引人注目的杀戮事件后试图恐吓目击者。新指南将结束联邦调查局对国土安全部特工袭击事件的调查。政府积极追查针对抗议者和其他人的案件，这些人甚至与特工有过短暂和无意识的身体接触。但根据《泰晤士报》的分析，这些重罪袭击案件通常都会失败，其中近一半以无罪判决告终，指控被驳回或撤回。虽然联邦调查局的调查表面上是为了帮助政府对抗议者和其他人立案，但他们经常提供针对警察的证据，这些证据可用于未来对他们的审判。据《泰晤士报》报道： > 根据这一变化，调查涉及联邦移民官员的案件的责任可能会落到 ICE 的一个部门国土安全调查局身上，实际上让该机构自行调查……国土安全调查局没有调查侵犯公民权利的管辖权，因此，将这一变化付诸实践将使 ICE 特工枪杀手无寸铁的人的事件不太可能被调查，以确定特工是否违反了联邦法律。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
